title: Codonoboea breviflora
intro: Codonoboea breviflora, also known as the "short-flowered Codonoboea," is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Codonoboea breviflora include iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Codonoboea breviflora is often made into a tea or infused into a medicinal decoction, and it is also used in the production of herbal extracts and capsules.
taxonomy: Codonoboea breviflora is a species of plant in the family Gesneriaceae. This family is commonly known as the African violet family. The Gesneriaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, including over 3,000 species. Codonoboea breviflora is native to Southeast Asia, specifically in the tropical forests of Malaysia and Indonesia. Within the Gesneriaceae family, Codonoboea breviflora is part of the genus Codonoboea, which includes several species of flowering plants.
